subtitle: How the Indus People Drew Order on the Earth

panels.shot: sweeping low-angle wide of a vast gridded city stretching to the horizon
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands on a high mud-brick platform overlooking straight streets, brick houses, and a distant reservoir under a hazy Bronze Age sun.

dialogue.text: In the age of bronze and river fog, the Harappan city builders drew straight lines upon the earth and taught a subcontinent how to live in planned streets.

i18n.hi: कांसे की उम्र और धुंधली नदियों में, हड़प्पा बसाने वाले धरती पर सीधी रेखाएँ खींचकर पूरे भूभाग को नियोजित गलियों में जीना सिखा गए।

panels.shot: wide aerial of the Indus basin and Ghaggar-Hakra region at dawn
panels.action: The Indus River twists through floodplains dotted with small village clusters, smoke from hearths rising into soft morning light.

dialogue.text: Before the great cities, there were fields and mud villages along many rivers, each with its own rhythm, each watching the floods rise and fall.

i18n.hi: महान शहरों से पहले, कई नदियों के किनारे खेत थे और कीचड़ के गाँव, हर एक की अपनी लय, हर एक बाढ़ के उठने‑गिरने को देखता हुआ।

panels.shot: medium shot from above of a work area with bricks being shaped
panels.action: Harappan City Builder kneels in a busy yard, measuring wet clay blocks with a simple cord as other workers stack drying bricks nearby.

dialogue.text: Then some hands began to measure clay. They cut bricks to a quiet rule, one to two to four, so that every block would answer another across the land.

i18n.hi: फिर कुछ हाथों ने मिट्टी नापनी शुरू की। उन्होंने ईंटें चुप नियम से काटीं—एक, दो, चार—ताकि हर गारा किसी दूर की ईंट का जवाब बन सके।

panels.shot: low wide shot of a brick kiln glowing at dusk
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands by a long updraft kiln mound as orange light glows from its vents, stacks of identical bricks waiting to be fired.

dialogue.speaker: Harappan City Builder
dialogue.text: If every brick is the same, every wall will know its place.

i18n.hi: जब हर ईंट एक सी हो, हर दीवार अपनी जगह जान लेती है।

panels.shot: bird's-eye view of a survey scene marked out with ropes and pegs
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands with assistants on a bare rise, stretching cords in straight lines across flattened ground while the sun casts long shadows.

dialogue.text: With straight cords and careful steps, they drew unseen lines first, making the earth itself a quiet drawing board for streets not yet built.

i18n.hi: सीधे डोर और नपे कदमों से उन्होंने पहले अदृश्य रेखाएँ खींचीं, धरती को ही आने वाली गलियों के लिए चुप चित्र‑पट बना दिया।

panels.shot: wide ground-level shot looking down a straight street under construction
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands at one end of a straight earthen lane, watching teams align foundation bricks into the first edges of a future street.

dialogue.text: At Harappa and Mohenjo-daro, streets rose to meet the compass. Main roads ran like rivers of dust, crossed by smaller lanes like ordered streams.

i18n.hi: हड़प्पा और मोहनजोदड़ो में सड़कें सीधे कंपास से मिलती उठीं। मुख्य रास्ते धूल की नदियों जैसे, और उनसे कटती छोटी गलियाँ, जैसे सधे हुए नाले।

panels.shot: cutaway-style wide shot showing higher citadel mound and lower town
panels.action: From a raised vantage, Harappan City Builder looks over a stepped mud-brick mound with solid structures above and a dense lower town spreading out below.

dialogue.text: They raised high mounds now called citadels, strong platforms against flood and time, and spread lower towns around them, each part knowing its work.

i18n.hi: उन्होंने ऊँचे टीले उठाए, जिन्हें आज दुर्ग कहते हैं—बाढ़ और समय के खिलाफ मजबूत चबूतरें—और उनके चारों ओर निचले शहर फैलाए, हर हिस्सा अपना काम जानता हुआ।

panels.shot: medium interior cutaway of a house and lane with drains
panels.action: Harappan City Builder inspects a lane where covered brick drains run beside house walls, while a woman pours water into a smaller household outlet.

dialogue.text: Inside this order, each house met the street with its own drain. Waste and water slipped away under brick covers, out of sight yet carefully guided.

i18n.hi: इस व्यवस्था के भीतर हर घर गली से अपने नाले के साथ मिलता था। गंदा पानी ईंटों से ढके रास्तों के नीचे से फिसलता, आँखों से दूर पर ध्यान से मोड़ा हुआ।

panels.shot: wide shot of the Great Bath area at Mohenjo-daro from above
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands near a large sunken brick tank lined with steps, surrounded by solid pillared structures on a high mound.

dialogue.text: On one high mound at Mohenjo-daro, they shaped a great brick tank. Its steps dropped into still water, fed and drained by hidden, careful channels.

i18n.hi: मोहनजोदड़ो के एक ऊँचे टीले पर उन्होंने ईंटों का विशाल कुंड तराशा। सीढ़ियाँ स्थिर जल में उतरतीं, छिपी, सधी नालियों से भरता और खाली होता हुआ।

panels.shot: sweeping aerial wide of Dholavira’s tripartite layout
panels.action: From above, Harappan City Builder overlooks Dholavira’s strong-walled citadel, a clearly bounded middle town, and a lower town, with huge reservoirs glinting nearby.

dialogue.text: Far to the west at Dholavira, the plan took a new shape: citadel, middle town, lower town, and vast water tanks set like mirrors around carved stone streets.

i18n.hi: दूर पश्चिम में धोलावीरा पर योजना ने नया रूप लिया—दुर्ग, मध्य नगर, निचला नगर, और विशाल जलकुंड, तराशी पत्थर की गलियों के चारों ओर दर्पणों से सजे।

panels.shot: medium shot near a massive stepped reservoir at Dholavira
panels.action: Harappan City Builder walks down broad stone steps into a deep dry reservoir, examining the smooth masonry walls that once held precious rainwater.

dialogue.speaker: Harappan City Builder
dialogue.text: Here, stone and empty depth will remember every short monsoon.

i18n.hi: यहाँ पत्थर और खाली गहराई हर छोटी मानसून को याद रखेंगे।

panels.shot: wide shot of Kalibangan with fortification and nearby ploughed field
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands on a mud-brick wall at Kalibangan, looking over a neatly furrowed field marked in a grid-like pattern beside the settlement.

dialogue.text: At Kalibangan, near the changing Ghaggar, they marked furrows in a field with care—an early memory of ploughing set beside a walled town.

i18n.hi: कालिबंगन में, बदलती घग्गर के पास, उन्होंने खेत में सावधानी से मेड़ें खींचीं—हल चलने की शुरुआती याद, दीवारों वाले नगर के बगल में दर्ज।

panels.shot: medium shot of an elevated platform area with several brick fire-altarlike structures
panels.action: Harappan City Builder examines a row of raised brick platforms with recessed designs, set neatly on a high area inside the settlement.

dialogue.text: On a high platform there, brick forms suggest careful rites, but their meaning is lost. The fire of their purpose has gone; only the pattern of bricks remains.

i18n.hi: वहाँ एक ऊँचे चबूतरें पर, ईंटों के आकार किसी सधे अनुष्ठान का संकेत देते हैं, पर अर्थ खो चुका है। उनकी मंशा की अग्नि बुझ गई; बस ईंटों की रेखाएँ बची हैं।

panels.shot: broad aerial of Rakhigarhi with excavated mounds and gridded streets
panels.action: Harappan City Builder walks along a high mound at Rakhigarhi, overlooking long straight streets and house blocks emerging from the dust.

dialogue.text: To the east at Rakhigarhi, another giant took form. Mounds spread wide, and as spades cut the earth in our own time, streets and drains reappeared like remembered lines.

i18n.hi: पूरब में राखीगढ़ी पर एक और दानव शहर उभर आया। टीले दूर‑दूर तक फैले, और जब हमारे समय की फावड़ियाँ धरती चीरती गईं, सड़कें और नालियाँ जैसे स्मृति से निकल फिर दिखने लगीं।

panels.shot: medium close-up of hands comparing standardized bricks and a cubical stone weight
panels.action: Harappan City Builder holds a standard 1:2:4 brick in one hand and a small cubical stone weight in the other, workers stacking identical items behind.

dialogue.text: From Sindh to Haryana, bricks kept their ratio, and stone weights their calm steps. Measures and modules travelled farther than any single ruler’s name.

i18n.hi: सिंध से हरियाणा तक ईंटों का अनुपात वही रहा, और पत्थर के बाट अपनी स्थिर चाल में। नाप‑तोल और माप‑खाने किसी भी एक शासक के नाम से कहीं आगे तक चले गए।

panels.shot: wide interior of a craft quarter with workshops opening onto a lane
panels.action: Harappan City Builder walks down a narrow lane where bead makers, potters, and metalworkers shape goods in open-fronted rooms aligned to the street.

dialogue.text: Craft streets grew along these lines—bead makers, potters, metal-workers. Each trade fit into the grid, their daily sounds woven into the planned silence of bricks.

i18n.hi: इन्हीं रेखाओं पर कारीगरों की गलियाँ उग आईं—मणिकार, कुम्हार, धातुकार। हर हुनर जाल में फिट, उनकी रोज़ की आवाज़ें ईंटों की सुनियोजित खामोशी में बुनी हुईं।

panels.shot: melancholic wide shot of partially abandoned streets with silt and encroaching sand
panels.action: Harappan City Builder stands alone in a quiet street, some doorways blocked with debris, wind-driven sand gathering at the base of brick walls.

dialogue.text: With time, rivers shifted, trade paths cooled, and some streets fell silent. The straight lines remained, but fewer feet traced them each season.

i18n.hi: समय के साथ नदियाँ मुड़ीं, व्यापार की राहें ठंडी पड़ीं, कुछ गलियाँ सुनी रह गईं। सीधी रेखाएँ रहीं, पर हर मौसम उन पर चलते कदम कम हो गए।

panels.shot: present-day wide shot of archaeologists working at a grid of excavation squares
panels.action: Modern archaeologists move carefully within square trenches cut into an ancient mound, revealing aligned walls and drains under the open sky.

dialogue.text: Thousands of years later, other hands cut neat squares into the mounds. Wall by wall, the old city plans rise again, their order still visible in the dust.

i18n.hi: हज़ारों साल बाद, दूसरी हथेलियों ने टीलों में सधे चौकोर काटे। दीवार‑दीवार, पुराने नगर‑योजन फिर उठ खड़े हुए, उनकी व्यवस्था अब भी धूल में साफ दिखती है।

panels.shot: slow push-in close-up on Harappan City Builder’s calm, weathered face fading into a city blueprint overlay
panels.action: Harappan City Builder’s thoughtful gaze fills the frame, dissolving into a faint overlay of gridded streets and brick courses stretching beyond him.

dialogue.text: The Harappan city builders left no known king’s name, only their measured lines. In every planned street that followed, their quiet lesson endures: build order into the very bricks of life.

i18n.hi: हड़प्पा नगर‑निर्माताओं ने किसी ज्ञात राजा का नाम नहीं छोड़ा, बस अपनी नापी‑तुली रेखाएँ। उनके बाद बनी हर नियोजित गली में उनका शांत सबक बचा है—ज़िंदगी की हर ईंट में व्यवस्था गढ़ो।

title_i18n.en: The City That Drew Itself in Straight Lines
title_i18n.hi: सीधी रेखाओं में खुद बनी शहर

subtitle_i18n.en: How the Indus People Drew Order on the Earth
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे सिंधु लोग धरती पर व्यवस्था रेखते थे
